text: I find myself in a liminal place, suspended between corridors of darkness and the promise of knowledge. The sterile, almost clinical ambiance of the hallway leads to a vibrant, intellectual space—an exhibit about research and development. The disorienting tilt compels me to question my bearings, reminding me how curiosity often arrives at odd angles in life. In this transition from the empty quiet to the color and thought of the display, I feel a sense of anticipation, as though I am on the threshold of discovery, standing at the intersection of the unknown and the explicable.
